<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kusahaka Kofun]][[en:Kusabaka Kofun]][[ja:艸墓古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990549]] Wikidata name: Kusahaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kusabaka Kofun | native_name =艸墓古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kusahaka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503208|N|135.845681|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|11.55|N|135|50|44.45|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of stratified [[:en:Hyaloclastite|Hyaloclastite]], which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kusahaka Kofun]][[en:Kusabaka Kofun]][[ja:艸墓古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kusahaka Kofun]][[en:Kusabaka Kofun]][[ja:艸墓古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17990549]] Wikidata name: Kusahaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kusabaka Kofun | native_name =艸墓古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kusahaka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503208|N|135.845681|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|11.55|N|135|50|44.45|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of stratified [[:en:Hyaloclastite|Hyaloclastite]], which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of stratified [[:en:Hyaloclastite|Hyaloclastite]], which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of stratified [[:en:Hyaloclastite|Hyaloclastite]], which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.

{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|WD=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite}}, which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|WD=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite}}, which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kusabaka Kofun | native_name =艸墓古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kusahaka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503208|N|135.845681|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|11.55|N|135|50|44.45|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|WD=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite|1=Hyaloclastite}}, which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.

The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|qid=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite|1=Hyaloclastite}}, which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|qid=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|qid=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kusabaka Kofun | native_name =艸墓古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kusahaka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusabaka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503208|N|135.845681|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|11.55|N|135|50|44.45|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kusabaka Kofun'''|艸墓古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202336|title=艸墓古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is also called the "Kusahaka Kofun".
==Overview== The Kusabaka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style rectangular tumulus located in the eastern edge of the Abe Hills on the left bank of the Terakawa River in the southern part of the Nara Basin. No [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted to date.The shape of the tumulus is rectangular, with the northwest and southeast sides being slightly longer, measuring approximately 27 meters on the long side and 21 meters on the short side. The top of the mound is flat and shaped like a truncated cone. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the outside of the mound, but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures have been found. The mound is surrounded by a flat area, but there is no trace of a moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is horizontal-entry style large stone chamber made of megaliths, with an opening to the southeast. The gaps between the stone walls were plastered, traces of which still remain today. Inside the chamber is a hollowed-out house-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of stratified {{ill|Hyaloclastite|en|Hyaloclastite|qid=Q964204|lt=Hyaloclastite|1=Hyaloclastite}}, which had been brought to this site from the [[Kakogawa River]] basin in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The cover stone has a pair of rope-hanging protrusions on the front and back and two pairs on the left and right, and the inside is shallowly carved. As the sarcophagus is larger than the entry passageway, it had to have been placed in the burial chamber during the construction of the tumulus. The burial chamber has been open since antiquity, and the existence of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. The burial chamber has a total length of 13.16 meters, with the main chamber measuring 4.44 by 2.71 meters, with a 2.0 meter height.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Based on its style of construction, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the mid-7th century, towards the end of the Kofun period. This tumulus is part of a cluster megalithic tombs in the Abe Hills. Remains of large buildings have also been found in the surrounding ruins, and connections to the ancient [[Abe clan]] have been speculated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sakurai Station (Nara)|en|Sakurai Station (Nara)|qid=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|qid=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is about a 30-minute walk from Sakurai Station on the Kintetsu Railway Osaka Line and other lines.
